            <command-message>plan</command-message>
            <command-args>there are some things that needs some fix please do fix all the issues without breaking anything please be careful \
\
\
\
1. Verdict

- Partial Pass

2. Scope and Verification Boundary

- Reviewed scope: static frontend repository under [repo](repo), including docs, scripts, routing, pages, modules, security, storage contracts, and tests.
- Excluded inputs: [./.tmp](.tmp) and all subpaths were not used as evidence.
- Not executed: project runtime, tests, Docker/container commands, build/dev/preview commands.
- Cannot statically confirm: real browser rendering quality, true runtime behavior of camera/media APIs, real timing fidelity under load, and UX polish.
- Manual verification required for: actual camera device compatibility, perceived visual hierarchy/spacing in browser, and real-world chunk upload throughput behavior.

3. Prompt / Repository Mapping Summary

- Prompt core business goals identified:
  - Offline warehouse + identity SPA with no backend.
  - Role-based access for Administrator, Warehouse Manager, Picker/Packer, Auditor.
  - Inventory + reservations + immutable ledger + waves/tasks/discrepancy closure.
  - File transfer with chunking, resumable behavior, dedup, rollback/retention, preview.
  - Identity enrollment with quality checks, liveness, vector generation, encrypted storage.
  - Local auth (PBKDF2), idle lock, AES-GCM for sensitive fields, masking/reveal model.
  - Offline notifications inbox + template/audit retries.
- Required pages and routing are statically present via [repo/frontend/src/routes/routes.ts](repo/frontend/src/routes/routes.ts#L22).
- Main-flow implementation areas reviewed:
  - App shell and route guard: [repo/frontend/src/App.svelte](repo/frontend/src/App.svelte#L1), [repo/frontend/src/app/route-guard.ts](repo/frontend/src/app/route-guard.ts#L1)
  - Inventory/orders/waves/files/identity/notifications routes: [repo/frontend/src/routes/Inventory.svelte](repo/frontend/src/routes/Inventory.svelte#L1), [repo/frontend/src/routes/Orders.svelte](repo/frontend/src/routes/Orders.svelte#L1), [repo/frontend/src/routes/Waves.svelte](repo/frontend/src/routes/Waves.svelte#L1), [repo/frontend/src/routes/Files.svelte](repo/frontend/src/routes/Files.svelte#L1), [repo/frontend/src/routes/Identity.svelte](repo/frontend/src/routes/Identity.svelte#L1), [repo/frontend/src/routes/Notifications.svelte](repo/frontend/src/routes/Notifications.svelte#L1)
  - Security/crypto/masking: [repo/frontend/src/lib/security/auth.service.ts](repo/frontend/src/lib/security/auth.service.ts#L1), [repo/frontend/src/lib/security/crypto.ts](repo/frontend/src/lib/security/crypto.ts#L1), [repo/frontend/src/lib/security/masking.ts](repo/frontend/src/lib/security/masking.ts#L1)
  - IndexedDB schema: [repo/frontend/src/lib/db/schema.ts](repo/frontend/src/lib/db/schema.ts#L1)

4. High / Blocker Coverage Panel

- A. Prompt-fit / completeness blockers: Partial Pass
- Reason: Most core domains are implemented and wired; however, Prompt-critical file transfer credibility is materially weakened by resumable-flow delivery gaps (see F-002).
- Evidence: [repo/frontend/src/routes/files/UploadModal.svelte](repo/frontend/src/routes/files/UploadModal.svelte#L45), [repo/frontend/src/modules/files/file.service.ts](repo/frontend/src/modules/files/file.service.ts#L104)
- Finding IDs: F-002

- B. Static delivery / structure blockers: Pass
- Reason: Documentation, scripts, entrypoints, routes, and architecture are broadly coherent and statically traceable.
- Evidence: [repo/README.md](repo/README.md#L1), [repo/frontend/package.json](repo/frontend/package.json#L1), [repo/frontend/src/main.ts](repo/frontend/src/main.ts#L1), [repo/frontend/src/routes/routes.ts](repo/frontend/src/routes/routes.ts#L1)

- C. Frontend-controllable interaction / state blockers: Fail
- Reason: Prompt-critical file chunk concurrency control is statically flawed; enforcement for max concurrent chunks is not credible.
- Evidence: [repo/frontend/src/modules/files/chunk-scheduler.ts](repo/frontend/src/modules/files/chunk-scheduler.ts#L112), [repo/frontend/src/modules/files/chunk-scheduler.ts](repo/frontend/src/modules/files/chunk-scheduler.ts#L118)
- Finding IDs: F-001

- D. Data exposure / delivery-risk blockers: Pass
- Reason: No real hardcoded tokens/secrets found; logging includes sanitization for sensitive keys.
- Evidence: [repo/frontend/src/lib/logging/logger.ts](repo/frontend/src/lib/logging/logger.ts#L15)

- E. Test-critical gaps: Partial Pass
- Reason: Test suite is broad, but critical chunk scheduler concurrency/resume behavior lacks direct behavioral validation and did not catch F-001.
- Evidence: [repo/frontend/unit_tests/services/file-transfer.test.ts](repo/frontend/unit_tests/services/file-transfer.test.ts#L106), [repo/frontend/src/modules/files/chunk-scheduler.ts](repo/frontend/src/modules/files/chunk-scheduler.ts#L112)
- Finding IDs: F-001

5. Confirmed Blocker / High Findings

- Finding ID: F-001
- Severity: High
- Conclusion: File chunk concurrency limit (max 3) is not credibly enforced due to broken pool-settlement logic.
- Brief rationale: The scheduler attempts to remove settled promises using a race against an immediately resolved false promise, which can make settled detection ineffective and allow broken throttling semantics.
- Evidence:
  - [repo/frontend/src/modules/files/chunk-scheduler.ts](repo/frontend/src/modules/files/chunk-scheduler.ts#L112)
  - [repo/frontend/src/modules/files/chunk-scheduler.ts](repo/frontend/src/modules/files/chunk-scheduler.ts#L113)
  - [repo/frontend/src/modules/files/chunk-scheduler.ts](repo/frontend/src/modules/files/chunk-scheduler.ts#L118)
  - [repo/frontend/src/modules/files/chunk-scheduler.ts](repo/frontend/src/modules/files/chunk-scheduler.ts#L120)
- Impact: Prompt requirement for 3-concurrent chunk transfer is not statically trustworthy; can break transfer predictability and professional delivery credibility.
- Minimum actionable fix: Replace the ad-hoc pool cleanup with a deterministic worker-pool or semaphore implementation that guarantees at most MAX_CONCURRENT_CHUNKS active tasks at any time, plus add a behavioral test that asserts active worker count never exceeds 3 during multi-chunk transfer.

- Finding ID: F-002
- Severity: High
- Conclusion: Resumable transfer capability is only partially implemented in service layer and not delivered as a credible user-facing resumable workflow.
- Brief rationale: UI upload path always starts a new ingest session; pause/resume in UI only toggles in-memory scheduler state; persisted paused sessions are not surfaced/recovered in screens.
- Evidence:
  - [repo/frontend/src/routes/files/UploadModal.svelte](repo/frontend/src/routes/files/UploadModal.svelte#L45)
  - [repo/frontend/src/routes/files/UploadModal.svelte](repo/frontend/src/routes/files/UploadModal.svelte#L95)
  - [repo/frontend/src/modules/files/file.service.ts](repo/frontend/src/modules/files/file.service.ts#L104)
  - [repo/frontend/src/modules/files/file.service.ts](repo/frontend/src/modules/files/file.service.ts#L123)
  - [repo/frontend/src/modules/files/file.store.ts](repo/frontend/src/modules/files/file.store.ts#L17)
  - [repo/frontend/src/modules/files/file.store.ts](repo/frontend/src/modules/files/file.store.ts#L9)
- Impact: Prompt-critical “resumable transfers” is not credibly delivered end-to-end in app UX/state flow, especially across interruption scenarios.
- Minimum actionable fix: Add transfer-session listing and resume controls in file-management UI, persist pause via service state transition, and implement startup/session recovery path that reloads incomplete sessions and allows explicit resume.

6. Other Findings Summary

- Severity: Medium
- Conclusion: README claims a Docker coverage variant that script does not implement.
- Evidence:
  - [repo/README.md](repo/README.md#L48)
  - [repo/run_tests.sh](repo/run_tests.sh#L10)
- Minimum actionable fix: Either parse and honor --coverage in [repo/run_tests.sh](repo/run_tests.sh#L1) or remove/update the README command.

- Severity: Medium
- Conclusion: Filter-chip scaffolding exists on some screens but lacks a corresponding “set filter” control, reducing Prompt-aligned filter interaction credibility.
- Evidence:
  - [repo/frontend/src/routes/Ledger.svelte](repo/frontend/src/routes/Ledger.svelte#L10)
  - [repo/frontend/src/routes/Ledger.svelte](repo/frontend/src/routes/Ledger.svelte#L41)
  - [repo/frontend/src/routes/Notifications.svelte](repo/frontend/src/routes/Notifications.svelte#L16)
  - [repo/frontend/src/routes/Notifications.svelte](repo/frontend/src/routes/Notifications.svelte#L64)
- Minimum actionable fix: Add filter selector/search inputs that set filter state and render chips as removable active filters.

7. Data Exposure and Delivery Risk Summary

- Real sensitive information exposure: Pass
- Evidence: No real credentials/API keys/tokens found in reviewed source; logger sanitizes sensitive keys in [repo/frontend/src/lib/logging/logger.ts](repo/frontend/src/lib/logging/logger.ts#L15).

- Hidden debug / config / demo-only surfaces: Partial Pass
- Evidence: No severe hidden debug surfaces found; however, transfer behavior has delivery-risk gaps (F-001, F-002) that can mask expected chunk/resume behavior.

- Undisclosed mock scope or default mock behavior: Pass
- Evidence: Docs clearly state no backend and local execution in [repo/README.md](repo/README.md#L1) and [docs/api-spec.md](docs/api-spec.md#L1).

- Fake-success or misleading delivery behavior: Partial Pass
- Evidence: File upload UI can present progress/completion path, but resumability is not credibly delivered end-to-end (F-002).

- Visible UI / console / storage leakage risk: Pass
- Evidence: Sensitive log fields are redacted in [repo/frontend/src/lib/logging/logger.ts](repo/frontend/src/lib/logging/logger.ts#L15); ordinary local business storage usage is appropriate for pure frontend constraints.

8. Test Sufficiency Summary

- Test Overview
- Unit tests exist: Yes, broad set under [repo/frontend/unit_tests](repo/frontend/unit_tests).
- Component tests exist: Yes, e.g. [repo/frontend/unit_tests/components/nav.test.ts](repo/frontend/unit_tests/components/nav.test.ts#L1).
- Page / route integration tests exist: Yes, e.g. [repo/frontend/unit_tests/integration/wiring.test.ts](repo/frontend/unit_tests/integration/wiring.test.ts#L1), [repo/frontend/unit_tests/routes.test.ts](repo/frontend/unit_tests/routes.test.ts#L1).
- E2E tests exist: Yes (jsdom-style smoke), [repo/frontend/e2e_tests/critical-path-smoke.test.ts](repo/frontend/e2e_tests/critical-path-smoke.test.ts#L1).
- Obvious test entry points: [repo/frontend/package.json](repo/frontend/package.json#L6), [repo/frontend/vite.config.ts](repo/frontend/vite.config.ts#L23), [repo/run_tests.sh](repo/run_tests.sh#L1).

- Core Coverage
- Happy path: covered
- Key failure paths: partially covered
- Interaction / state coverage: partially covered

- Major Gaps
- Missing behavioral test proving chunk scheduler never exceeds 3 concurrent active chunk tasks during real schedule run: [repo/frontend/unit_tests/services/file-transfer.test.ts](repo/frontend/unit_tests/services/file-transfer.test.ts#L106)
- Missing test for persisted paused transfer recovery and user-driven resume flow from file screen: [repo/frontend/src/modules/files/file.store.ts](repo/frontend/src/modules/files/file.store.ts#L17)
- Missing UI-level test for resumable transfer list/actions in files route (no route-level evidence found): [repo/frontend/src/routes/Files.svelte](repo/frontend/src/routes/Files.svelte#L1)
- Limited route-screen assertions for active filter-setting interactions on ledger/notifications: [repo/frontend/src/routes/Ledger.svelte](repo/frontend/src/routes/Ledger.svelte#L41)
- No evidence of end-to-end assertion for pause persistence status transitions in UI flow: [repo/frontend/src/routes/files/UploadModal.svelte](repo/frontend/src/routes/files/UploadModal.svelte#L95)

- Final Test Verdict
- Partial Pass

9. Engineering Quality Summary

- Architecture is generally coherent and modular (routes/components/modules/services/db/security separation is clear).
- Major maintainability risk is concentrated in file transfer orchestration correctness and incomplete resumable delivery path (F-001, F-002).
- Outside those areas, separation and domain organization are credible for a pure frontend offline application.

10. Visual and Interaction Summary

- Static structure supports a plausible application layout with nav rail, top search, tables, drawers/modals, and page segmentation via route modules.
- Static code supports presence of loading/empty/error messaging in key components (for example DataTable + page-level error blocks).
- Cannot confirm final visual quality, responsiveness polish, transition behavior, and true interaction affordance quality without execution/manual verification.
- Static weakness observed: some filter-chip usage lacks visible filter-setting controls (Medium).

11. Next Actions
1. Fix chunk scheduler concurrency control with deterministic worker/semaphore logic; add strict max-active assertion test (F-001).
1. Deliver true resumable transfer UX: persisted paused/incomplete session list, resume action, and startup recovery wiring (F-002).
1. Add integration tests that cover pause persistence and resume after interruption from the files route.
1. Add behavioral tests for scheduler progress and active-count bounds under multi-chunk uploads.
1. Resolve README vs script coverage-command mismatch.
1. Add actionable filter controls where filter chips are rendered (ledger/notifications).</command-args>

1	# ForgeOps Offline Warehouse & Identity Console
2	
3	Offline browser SPA for warehouse inventory, orders, file management, and identity — built with Svelte 4 + TypeScript, Vite 5, IndexedDB, and WebCrypto. No backend required.
4	
5	## Prerequisites
6	
7	- Node.js 20+
8	- npm 9+
9	- Docker (optional, for containerized preview and `run_tests.sh`)
10	
11	## Setup
12	
13	```bash
14	cd repo/frontend
15	npm install
16	```
17	
18	## Run the Application
19	
20	```bash
21	# Development server — http://localhost:5173
22	npm run dev
23	
24	# Production build
25	npm run build
26	
27	# Preview production build — http://localhost:4173
28	npm run preview
29	```
30	
31	## Run Tests
32	
33	```bash
34	# Run all tests
35	npm run test
36	
37	# Watch mode
38	npm run test:watch
39	
40	# With coverage report
41	npm run test:coverage
42	```
43	
44	Or from the repo root (requires Docker and Docker Compose):
45	
46	```bash
47	bash run_tests.sh            # run tests inside Docker container
48	bash run_tests.sh --coverage # run with coverage inside Docker container
49	```
50	
51	> **Note:** `run_tests.sh` uses `docker compose` under the hood. For local testing without Docker, use `npm run test` from the `repo/frontend` directory.
52	
53	## Docker
54	
55	```bash
56	# From repo/ directory
57	docker compose up --build
58	
59	# App available at http://localhost:4173
60	```
61	
62	## Scripts
63	
64	| Script | Description |
65	|--------|-------------|
66	| `npm run dev` | Dev server (port 5173) |
67	| `npm run build` | Production build |
68	| `npm run preview` | Preview build (port 4173) |
69	| `npm run test` | Run unit tests |
70	| `npm run test:coverage` | Tests + v8 coverage |
71	| `npm run check` | TypeScript type checking |
72	
73	## Configuration
74	
75	- **Ports**: 5173 (dev), 4173 (preview/Docker)
76	- **Persistence**: IndexedDB for data, LocalStorage for UI preferences
77	- **Security**: PBKDF2 password hashing, AES-GCM encryption via WebCrypto
78	- **No env variables required** — all defaults are built in
79	- **Fully offline** — no network calls, no backend API
80	
81	## Documentation
82	
83	- [`docs/design.md`](../docs/design.md) — Architecture and design
84	- [`docs/api-spec.md`](../docs/api-spec.md) — Local service contracts
85	- [`docs/test-traceability.md`](../docs/test-traceability.md) — Requirement-to-test mapping

1	# ForgeOps Offline Warehouse & Identity Console
2	
3	Offline browser SPA for warehouse inventory, orders, file management, and identity — built with Svelte 4 + TypeScript, Vite 5, IndexedDB, and WebCrypto. No backend required.
4	
5	## Prerequisites
6	
7	- Node.js 20+
8	- npm 9+
9	- Docker (optional, for containerized preview and `run_tests.sh`)
10	
11	## Setup
12	
13	```bash
14	cd repo/frontend
15	npm install
16	```
17	
18	## Run the Application
19	
20	```bash
21	# Development server — http://localhost:5173
22	npm run dev
23	
24	# Production build
25	npm run build
26	
27	# Preview production build — http://localhost:4173
28	npm run preview
29	```
30	
31	## Run Tests
32	
33	```bash
34	# Run all tests
35	npm run test
36	
37	# Watch mode
38	npm run test:watch
39	
40	# With coverage report
41	npm run test:coverage
42	```
43	
44	Or from the repo root (requires Docker and Docker Compose):
45	
46	```bash
47	bash run_tests.sh            # run tests inside Docker container
48	bash run_tests.sh --coverage # run with coverage inside Docker container
49	```
50	
51	> **Note:** `run_tests.sh` uses `docker compose` under the hood. For local testing without Docker, use `npm run test` from the `repo/frontend` directory.
52	
53	## Docker
54	
55	```bash
56	# From repo/ directory
57	docker compose up --build
58	
59	# App available at http://localhost:4173
60	```
61	
62	## Scripts
63	
64	| Script | Description |
65	|--------|-------------|
66	| `npm run dev` | Dev server (port 5173) |
67	| `npm run build` | Production build |
68	| `npm run preview` | Preview build (port 4173) |
69	| `npm run test` | Run unit tests |
70	| `npm run test:coverage` | Tests + v8 coverage |
71	| `npm run check` | TypeScript type checking |
72	
73	## Configuration
74	
75	- **Ports**: 5173 (dev), 4173 (preview/Docker)
76	- **Persistence**: IndexedDB for data, LocalStorage for UI preferences
77	- **Security**: PBKDF2 password hashing, AES-GCM encryption via WebCrypto
78	- **No env variables required** — all defaults are built in
79	- **Fully offline** — no network calls, no backend API
80	
81	## Documentation
82	
83	- [`docs/design.md`](../docs/design.md) — Architecture and design
84	- [`docs/api-spec.md`](../docs/api-spec.md) — Local service contracts
85	- [`docs/test-traceability.md`](../docs/test-traceability.md) — Requirement-to-test mapping

{"description": "Design implementation plan", "subagent_type": "Plan", "prompt": "I need a detailed implementation plan to fix 6 issues identified in an audit of a Svelte/TypeScript offline warehouse SPA. Here's everything I know:

## Issue F-001: Broken chunk scheduler concurrency control (HIGH)

**File**: `repo/frontend/src/modules/files/chunk-scheduler.ts` (lines 104-123)

The current promise pool logic is broken. The code tries to remove settled promises from the pool using:
```typescript
if (pool.length >= MAX_CONCURRENT_CHUNKS) {
  await Promise.race(pool);
  // Remove completed promises
  for (let i = pool.length - 1; i >= 0; i--) {
    const settled = await Promise.race([
      pool[i].then(() => true),
      Promise.resolve(false),
    ]);
    if (settled) pool.splice(i, 1);
  }
}
```

The `Promise.race([pool[i].then(() => true), Promise.resolve(false)])` always resolves to `false` immediately because `Promise.resolve(false)` is already resolved and wins the race. This means settled promises are never removed from the pool, breaking concurrency control.

**Fix approach**: Replace with a proper semaphore/worker-pool pattern. Each promise in the pool should self-remove when it settles. A clean approach:
- Wrap each promise so it removes itself from the pool on completion
- Use `Promise.race(pool)` to wait for any one to complete before adding next
- The key insight: each promise should carry its own identity for removal

```typescript
const pool: Set<Promise<void>> = new Set();
for (const index of pendingIndices) {
  if (this.isPaused) break;
  const promise = processNext(index).then(() => {
    pool.delete(promise);
  });
  pool.add(promise);
  if (pool.size >= MAX_CONCURRENT_CHUNKS) {
    await Promise.race(pool);
  }
}
await Promise.all(pool);
```

## Issue F-002: Resumable transfer not delivered end-to-end (HIGH)

**Current state**:
- `UploadModal.svelte` always starts a new ingest session (line 45: `ingestFile(...)`)
- Pause/resume only toggles in-memory scheduler state (lines 95-103)
- `file.store.ts` has `transferStore` and `loadTransfers()` but these aren't used in the Files page
- `TransferSessionRepository` has `getByStatus(status)` method
- `file.service.ts` has `pauseTransfer()`, `resumeTransfer()` that persist state to IndexedDB
- The `scheduleChunks` method already checks for existing completed chunks (`completedIndices`) and only processes pending ones

**What needs to happen**:
1. `UploadModal.svelte` pause button should call `pauseTransfer()` to persist paused status to DB (not just in-memory)
2. `Files.svelte` needs a section showing incomplete/paused transfers from IndexedDB, with resume controls
3. Resume action should: load transfer session from DB, get file data (need to think about this - file data is in chunks already stored, but remaining data needs the original file), call `resumeTransfer()` to update DB state, then call `scheduleChunks()` which already handles partial completion

**Key challenge**: The `scheduleChunks` method needs the `fileData: ArrayBuffer` to slice into chunks. On resume from a fresh page load, we don't have the original ArrayBuffer. We need the user to re-select the file, then we can resume from where we left off.

**Realistic approach**:
- Show paused/incomplete transfers in Files.svelte
- Allow user to re-attach the file to resume (re-select the file)
- Call `scheduleChunks` which already skips completed chunks
- On UploadModal pause, persist session state via `pauseTransfer()`

## Issue: README vs run_tests.sh coverage mismatch (MEDIUM)

**README.md line 48**: `bash run_tests.sh --coverage`
**run_tests.sh**: Only 14 lines, just runs `docker compose --profile test run --rm --build test`, doesn't parse any args.
**docker-compose.yml test service**: Just uses `Dockerfile.test`

**Fix**: Add argument parsing to `run_tests.sh` to forward `--coverage` flag. Need to check what the test Dockerfile runs - likely `npm run test`, so we can switch to `npm run test:coverage` when flag is passed.

## Issue: Filter chips without filter controls on Ledger and Notifications (MEDIUM)

**Ledger.svelte**: Has `reasonFilter` state and FilterChip showing when set, but no way to set it from UI. The data has a `reasonCode` column. Need a dropdown/select to choose a reason code from available values.

**Notifications.svelte**: Has `typeFilter` state and FilterChip showing when set, but no way to set it. The `NotificationType` enum exists with values. Need a dropdown/select to choose a notification type.

**Approach**: Add a `<select>` element in the filters area for each page. For Ledger, derive unique reason codes from the data. For Notifications, use the `NotificationType` enum values.

## Test gaps to fill

**File**: `repo/frontend/unit_tests/services/file-transfer.test.ts`

1. **Concurrency behavioral test**: Need a test that actually runs `scheduleChunks()` with enough chunks and asserts `getActiveCount()` never exceeds `MAX_CONCURRENT_CHUNKS`.
2. **Pause persistence test**: Test that pausing via the service persists the paused state and resuming works.

## Existing patterns/utilities to reuse

- `FilterChip` component (already imported in both pages)
- `TransferSessionRepository.getByStatus()` - for querying paused/incomplete sessions
- `loadTransfers()` from `file.store.ts` - for loading transfer sessions
- `transferStore` from `file.store.ts` - writable store for transfer sessions
- `pauseTransfer()` / `resumeTransfer()` from `file.service.ts` - already persist to DB
- `TransferState` enum - Pending, Active, Paused, Completed, Failed
- `NotificationType` enum - for notification filter options
- `MovementReason` enum - likely available for ledger filter options (need to verify)

Please provide a detailed step-by-step implementation plan with the exact changes needed for each file, organized by issue."}

## Issue F-001: Broken chunk scheduler concurrency control (HIGH)

**File**: `repo/frontend/src/modules/files/chunk-scheduler.ts` (lines 104-123)

The current promise pool logic is broken. The code tries to remove settled promises from the pool using:
```typescript
if (pool.length >= MAX_CONCURRENT_CHUNKS) {
  await Promise.race(pool);
  // Remove completed promises
  for (let i = pool.length - 1; i >= 0; i--) {
    const settled = await Promise.race([
      pool[i].then(() => true),
      Promise.resolve(false),
    ]);
    if (settled) pool.splice(i, 1);
  }
}
```

The `Promise.race([pool[i].then(() => true), Promise.resolve(false)])` always resolves to `false` immediately because `Promise.resolve(false)` is already resolved and wins the race. This means settled promises are never removed from the pool, breaking concurrency control.

**Fix approach**: Replace with a proper semaphore/worker-pool pattern. Each promise in the pool should self-remove when it settles. A clean approach:
- Wrap each promise so it removes itself from the pool on completion
- Use `Promise.race(pool)` to wait for any one to complete before adding next
- The key insight: each promise should carry its own identity for removal

for (const index of pendingIndices) {

    pool.delete(promise);
  });
  pool.add(promise);
  if (pool.size >= MAX_CONCURRENT_CHUNKS) {

}

{"file_path": "C:\Users\Hello\.claude\plans\cheeky-wishing-acorn.md", "content": "# Fix Plan: Audit Findings F-001, F-002, and Medium Issues

## Context

An audit identified 2 High and 3 Medium issues in the ForgeOps Offline Warehouse SPA. The chunk scheduler's concurrency control is broken (promises never removed from pool), resumable file transfers aren't delivered end-to-end in the UI, filter pages have chips without filter controls, and the README documents a `--coverage` flag that `run_tests.sh` doesn't support. This plan fixes all issues and adds missing tests.

---

## 1. F-001: Fix Chunk Scheduler Concurrency Control (HIGH)

**File**: `repo/frontend/src/modules/files/chunk-scheduler.ts`

**Bug**: Lines 112-122 — The pool cleanup uses `Promise.race([pool[i].then(() => true), Promise.resolve(false)])`. Since `Promise.resolve(false)` is already resolved, it always wins the race, so `settled` is always `false` and completed promises are never removed. The pool grows unbounded, breaking the MAX_CONCURRENT_CHUNKS=3 limit.

**Fix**: Replace lines 104-126 (the array-based pool) with a `Set`-based self-removing pattern:

```typescript
const pool: Set<Promise<void>> = new Set();
for (const index of pendingIndices) {
  if (this.isPaused) break;

  const promise = processNext(index).then(
    () => { pool.delete(promise); },
    () => { pool.delete(promise); },
  );
  pool.add(promise);

  if (pool.size >= MAX_CONCURRENT_CHUNKS) {
    await Promise.race(pool);
  }
}

await Promise.all(pool);
```

Each promise removes itself from the set on settle (success or error). `Promise.race` then correctly blocks until a slot opens. No public API changes.

---

## 2. F-002: Deliver Resumable Transfers End-to-End (HIGH)

### Part A — Persist pause state in UploadModal

**File**: `repo/frontend/src/routes/files/UploadModal.svelte`

Changes:
1. Add `pauseTransfer`, `resumeTransfer` to the import from `$modules/files` (line 5)
2. Add `let sessionId: string | null = null` state variable
3. Store `sessionId = result.session.id` after session creation (around line 58)
4. Make `handlePause()` async and call `pauseTransfer(sessionId)` / `resumeTransfer(sessionId)` to persist state to IndexedDB
5. After `scheduleChunks` returns, check if `paused` — if so, show toast "Upload paused. Resume from the Files page.", skip `completeTransfer`, and close modal

### Part B — Show incomplete transfers with resume controls in Files page

**File**: `repo/frontend/src/routes/Files.svelte`

Changes:
1. Add imports: `transferStore`, `loadTransfers`, `resumeTransfer`, `getTransferSession`, `completeTransfer`, `getFile`, `ChunkScheduler` from `$modules/files`; `TransferState` from enums; `PreferenceStorage` from preferences
2. In `onMount`, also call `loadTransfers()`
3. Derive `incompleteTransfers` reactively: filter `$transferStore` for `Paused` or `Active` status
4. Resolve file names for display using `getFile(t.fileId)` after loading transfers
5. Add a resume handler: accepts session ID + file re-selection event, creates new `ChunkScheduler`, calls `resumeTransfer()` then `scheduleChunks()` (which skips already-completed chunks), then `completeTransfer()`
6. Add UI section above the DataTable showing incomplete transfers with file name, progress (completedChunks/totalChunks), status badge, and a file-input "Resume" button

---

## 3. Filter Controls for Ledger and Notifications (MEDIUM)

### Ledger.svelte

**File**: `repo/frontend/src/routes/Ledger.svelte`

1. Import `MovementReason` from `$lib/types/enums`
2. Add a `<select>` in the `.filters` div bound to `reasonFilter`, with `<option value="">All Reasons</option>` and options from `Object.values(MovementReason)`
3. Add `.filter-select` styling

### Notifications.svelte

**File**: `repo/frontend/src/routes/Notifications.svelte`

1. `NotificationType` is already imported (line 8)
2. Add a `<select>` in the `.filters` div bound to `typeFilter`, with `<option value="">All Types</option>` and options from `Object.values(NotificationType)`
3. Add `.filter-select` styling

---

## 4. README vs run_tests.sh Coverage Mismatch (MEDIUM)

**File**: `repo/run_tests.sh`

Add `--coverage` argument handling. When passed, override the Docker CMD:

```bash
if [[ "${1:-}" == "--coverage" ]]; then
  docker compose --profile test run --rm --build test npm run test:coverage
else
  docker compose --profile test run --rm --build test
fi
```

No changes to `docker-compose.yml` or `Dockerfile.test` — `docker compose run` appends args as the command override.

---

## 5. Add Missing Tests (MEDIUM)

**File**: `repo/frontend/unit_tests/services/file-transfer.test.ts`

### Test A: Concurrency behavioral assertion

Inside the `ChunkScheduler` describe block, add a test that:
- Creates a file with `MAX_CONCURRENT_CHUNKS * 3` chunks (9 chunks)
- Spies on `processChunk` with a small async delay (`setTimeout 5ms`)
- Tracks peak `getActiveCount()` during execution
- Asserts peak never exceeds `MAX_CONCURRENT_CHUNKS` (3)
- Asserts all chunks were stored in DB

### Test B: Pause persistence and resume from checkpoint

Inside the `ChunkScheduler` describe block, add a test that:
- Creates a 6-chunk file, spies on `processChunk` to call `scheduler.pause()` after 2 chunks
- Runs `scheduleChunks` (stops early due to pause)
- Calls `pauseTransfer()` and verifies DB state is `Paused`
- Verifies partial chunks exist (>0 but <6)
- Calls `resumeTransfer()`, creates a fresh `ChunkScheduler`, runs `scheduleChunks` again
- Verifies all 6 chunks now exist

---

## Files Modified (Summary)

| File | Issue |
|------|-------|
| `repo/frontend/src/modules/files/chunk-scheduler.ts` | F-001 |
| `repo/frontend/src/routes/files/UploadModal.svelte` | F-002 Part A |
| `repo/frontend/src/routes/Files.svelte` | F-002 Part B |
| `repo/frontend/src/routes/Ledger.svelte` | Filter controls |
| `repo/frontend/src/routes/Notifications.svelte` | Filter controls |
| `repo/run_tests.sh` | Coverage flag |
| `repo/frontend/unit_tests/services/file-transfer.test.ts` | Test gaps |

## Execution Order

1. F-001 — chunk-scheduler.ts (no dependencies)
2. Tests for F-001 — concurrency behavioral test
3. F-002 Part A — UploadModal.svelte pause persistence
4. F-002 Part B — Files.svelte resume UI
5. Tests for F-002 — pause/resume test
6. Filter controls — Ledger.svelte + Notifications.svelte (independent)
7. run_tests.sh — coverage flag (independent)

## Verification

1. Run `npm run test` from `repo/frontend` to verify all existing + new tests pass
2. Confirm the concurrency test asserts peak active count <= 3
3. Confirm the pause/resume test verifies partial chunks, DB state transition, and full completion on resume
4. Visually inspect Ledger and Notifications pages have working `<select>` filter dropdowns
5. Run `bash run_tests.sh --coverage` to verify the flag is honored
"}

{"plan": "# Fix Plan: Audit Findings F-001, F-002, and Medium Issues

## Context

An audit identified 2 High and 3 Medium issues in the ForgeOps Offline Warehouse SPA. The chunk scheduler's concurrency control is broken (promises never removed from pool), resumable file transfers aren't delivered end-to-end in the UI, filter pages have chips without filter controls, and the README documents a `--coverage` flag that `run_tests.sh` doesn't support. This plan fixes all issues and adds missing tests.

## 1. F-001: Fix Chunk Scheduler Concurrency Control (HIGH)

**File**: `repo/frontend/src/modules/files/chunk-scheduler.ts`

**Bug**: Lines 112-122 — The pool cleanup uses `Promise.race([pool[i].then(() => true), Promise.resolve(false)])`. Since `Promise.resolve(false)` is already resolved, it always wins the race, so `settled` is always `false` and completed promises are never removed. The pool grows unbounded, breaking the MAX_CONCURRENT_CHUNKS=3 limit.

**Fix**: Replace lines 104-126 (the array-based pool) with a `Set`-based self-removing pattern:

```typescript
const pool: Set<Promise<void>> = new Set();
for (const index of pendingIndices) {
  if (this.isPaused) break;

  const promise = processNext(index).then(
    () => { pool.delete(promise); },
    () => { pool.delete(promise); },
  );
  pool.add(promise);

  if (pool.size >= MAX_CONCURRENT_CHUNKS) {
    await Promise.race(pool);
  }
}

await Promise.all(pool);
```

Each promise removes itself from the set on settle (success or error). `Promise.race` then correctly blocks until a slot opens. No public API changes.

## Issue F-001: Broken chunk scheduler concurrency control (HIGH)

**File**: `repo/frontend/src/modules/files/chunk-scheduler.ts` (lines 104-123)

The current promise pool logic is broken. The code tries to remove settled promises from the pool using:
```typescript
if (pool.length >= MAX_CONCURRENT_CHUNKS) {
  await Promise.race(pool);
  // Remove completed promises
  for (let i = pool.length - 1; i >= 0; i--) {
    const settled = await Promise.race([
      pool[i].then(() => true),
      Promise.resolve(false),
    ]);
    if (settled) pool.splice(i, 1);
  }
}
```

The `Promise.race([pool[i].then(() => true), Promise.resolve(false)])` always resolves to `false` immediately because `Promise.resolve(false)` is already resolved and wins the race. This means settled promises are never removed from the pool, breaking concurrency control.

**Fix approach**: Replace with a proper semaphore/worker-pool pattern. Each promise in the pool should self-remove when it settles. A clean approach:
- Wrap each promise so it removes itself from the pool on completion
- Use `Promise.race(pool)` to wait for any one to complete before adding next
- The key insight: each promise should carry its own identity for removal

```typescript
const pool: Set<Promise<void>> = new Set();
for (const index of pendingIndices) {
  if (this.isPaused) break;
  const promise = processNext(index).then(() => {
    pool.delete(promise);
  });
  pool.add(promise);
  if (pool.size >= MAX_CONCURRENT_CHUNKS) {
    await Promise.race(pool);
  }
}
await Promise.all(pool);
```

1. `UploadModal.svelte` pause button should call `pauseTransfer()` to persist paused status to DB (not just in-memory)
2. `Files.svelte` needs a section showing incomplete/paused transfers from IndexedDB, with resume controls
3. Resume action should: load transfer session from DB, get file data (need to think about this - file data is in chunks already stored, but remaining data needs the original file), call `resumeTransfer()` to update DB state, then call `scheduleChunks()` which already handles partial completion

**Key challenge**: The `scheduleChunks` method needs the `fileData: ArrayBuffer` to slice into chunks. On resume from a fresh page load, we don't have the original ArrayBuffer. We need the user to re-select the file, then we can resume from where we left off.
